name: Indus Hospital
mission:
Friends of Indus Hospital, Inc (FOIH) is a US-based 501(c)(3) registered charity. We strive to create awareness about the healthcare needs of underprivileged patients and raise funds to provide them with equitable care. By supporting healthcare efforts in Pakistan, FOIH is helping to ensure that vulnerable individuals have access to the healthcare they need, regardless of financial or other barriers.
Through annual fundraising initiatives, community events, and awareness drives, we seek to mobilize a strong tradition of community giving and a shared commitment to changing the paradigm of healthcare delivery in Pakistan.
FOIH is an initiative taken by Americans of Pakistani origin expatriates and supported by a wider community across the USA.
All donations given to FOIH are eligible for tax deduction under US law.
scoreSummary: Friends of Indus Hospital Inc shows strong alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Zakat donations are crucial in supporting these initiatives. As a registered 501(c)(3) organization, donations made through FOIH are eligible for tax deductions, and zakat contributions are fully utilized to provide free healthcare services to those in need. (Source: https://www.feelingblessed.org/foihus)

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a strong overall rating due to its high financial efficiency and direct service model in a high-urgency humanitarian sector. Its Zakat-eligible status and high alignment with donor values further bolster its standing.
areas_for_improvement: Relatively low working capital of 1.8 months, which may limit short-term financial flexibility.
Opportunity to provide more detailed cost-per-beneficiary data to enhance impact transparency.

dimension_explanations.alignment: Friends of Indus Hospital Inc shows high alignment with humanitarian and religious donor values, specifically through its Zakat-eligible status and Shariah-compliant donation options [1][3]. The cause is considered high urgency due to the critical need for accessible healthcare in Pakistan [4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates strong impact through direct service programs including prosthetic limbs, neonatal care, and safe birth initiatives [4]. With a total revenue of $14,690,150 and a program expense ratio of 86.8%, it effectively converts donations into tangible healthcare services [2][4].

baseline_narrative.headline: Friends of Indus Hospital Inc supports a vital healthcare network providing free, high-quality medical services to underserved populations in Pakistan.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 86.8%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach medical programs.
Excellent fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.11 per $1 raised.
Verified Zakat-eligible status with clear policies for Shariah-compliant giving.
baseline_narrative.summary: Friends of Indus Hospital Inc is a U.S.-based non-profit dedicated to supporting the Indus Hospital and Health Network's mission of providing premium healthcare at no cost to patients in Pakistan [4]. The organization maintains a high Charity Navigator score of 95.75/100, reflecting its commitment to transparency and financial accountability [2].

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support high-quality, free healthcare in Pakistan through a Zakat-compliant and highly transparent U.S. entity.

Value high transparency and top-tier charity ratings
Prefer organizations with low fundraising overhead
Care about healthcare access for the underserved in Pakistan
giving_considerations: Donations support a large hospital network rather than a single clinic
The organization operates with lean reserves, making consistent giving valuable
Impact is measured through service delivery metrics rather than RCTs
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fund medical research or those looking for local U.S.-based healthcare initiatives.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects consistent tracking of <cite id="16">6 outcome metrics</cite> but lacks a formal third-party impact evaluation.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The charity provides free healthcare to remove financial barriers, leading to improved health outcomes for the poor in Pakistan.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking metrics ensures that hospital resources are effectively reaching the intended patient populations.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="13">24.1% revenue growth rate</cite>, the charity is scaling to meet massive unmet healthcare needs in Pakistan.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by an <cite id="5">exceptionally low fundraising cost</cite> compared to the peer median.

strengths.detail: The charity spends only <cite id="5">$0.11 per $1 raised</cite>, significantly outperforming many international relief peers.
The organization has earned a <cite id="3">95.75/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="8">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>.
FOIH supports <cite id="9">direct healthcare services</cite> including primary care and specialized oncology for those in need.

strengths_deep_dive: Friends of Indus Hospital demonstrates a strong commitment to financial stewardship, evidenced by its <cite id="10">86.8% program expense ratio</cite> reported by Charity Navigator. This efficiency is paired with high governance marks, including a <cite id="11">100% independent board of directors</cite>. The organization's ability to maintain <cite id="12">perfect scores of 100 in Culture and Leadership</cite> beacons suggests a stable and well-managed internal environment. Furthermore, their <cite id="13">3-year revenue CAGR of 24.1%</cite> indicates a rapidly growing donor base that trusts the organization's ability to scale healthcare interventions in Pakistan.
rich_narrative.summary: Friends of Indus Hospital Inc (FOIH) serves as a critical financial engine for the Indus Hospital and Health Network, which provides <cite id="1">free, high-quality medical services</cite> to underserved populations across Pakistan. The organization operates on a model of universal access, ensuring that no patient is turned away due to an inability to pay. This mission is supported by a robust fundraising infrastructure in the United States, which generated <cite id="2">$14,742,480 in total revenue</cite> during the 2023 fiscal year. The charity maintains a <cite id="3">95.75/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, signaling a high level of institutional trust and operational transparency. By focusing on direct service delivery, FOIH enables specialized care in areas such as <cite id="4">childhood cancer, neonatal health, and safe birthing</cite>. Their financial model is notably efficient, spending only <cite id="5">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite> in donations. This lean approach ensures that the vast majority of donor contributions are directed toward medical supplies, hospital infrastructure, and clinical staff in Pakistan. For Muslim donors, the organization offers a <cite id="6">verified Zakat-eligible giving pathway</cite>, with funds specifically designated for the <cite id="7">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> who seek treatment at Indus facilities.
